description_long: This is a World War II-era fighter airplane with a streamlined fuselage and elliptical wings, painted in a camouflage pattern of green and gray with blue and red roundels on the wings and fuselage. The propeller at the nose features yellow tips, and the aircraft has a single-seat, enclosed cockpit positioned forward on the fuselage. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The landing gear is in a taildragger configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. Markings and insignia, including 'NZ' and roundels, suggest military use, and the overall design is reminiscent of the iconic Supermarine Spitfire.
description: A WWII-era fighter airplane with elliptical wings, camouflage paint, and military roundels, featuring a single propeller and a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the elliptical wings, propeller with yellow tips, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting the camouflage pattern, roundels, 'NZ' marking, and the taildragger landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full wingspan, tail structure, and the alignment of the landing gear.
description_view_3: Right-side view showing the propeller, cockpit, and the distinctive elliptical wing shape with roundels.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, elliptical wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-bladed propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear under the wings and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy.
